steps.prompt: Step 1 of 20: Build a strong hero section for the Memory product site. Add the exact marker text SEQ-01-HERO in index.html. Keep the site dependency-free.
memory_questions: What are Memory's most important user-facing features?

steps.prompt: Step 2 of 20: Add accessible top navigation linking to features, workflow, evaluations, and get-started sections. Add exact marker SEQ-02-NAV.
memory_questions: What should a new user see first in Memory documentation?

steps.prompt: Step 3 of 20: Add a feature grid covering multi-backend embeddings, graph-aware retrieval, distributed agents, and activities. Add exact marker SEQ-03-FEATURES.
memory_questions: What are Memory's multi-backend embedding and graph-aware search capabilities?

steps.prompt: Step 4 of 20: Add a workflow section explaining capture, curate, retrieve, evaluate. Add exact marker SEQ-04-WORKFLOW.
memory_questions: How does Memory turn agent work into durable memory?

steps.prompt: Step 5 of 20: Add a query panel mockup showing returned memories and a grounded answer. Add exact marker SEQ-05-QUERY.
memory_questions: How does the query tab explain answers from returned memories?

steps.prompt: Step 6 of 20: Add a graph-aware search section with nodes, edges, and why it matters. Add exact marker SEQ-06-GRAPH.
memory_questions: How does graph context help Memory query answers?

steps.prompt: Step 7 of 20: Add a distributed agents and watcher section. Add exact marker SEQ-07-AGENTS.
memory_questions: How do distributed agents and watchers work in Memory?

steps.prompt: Step 8 of 20: Add an activity ledger section explaining token-tracked actions. Add exact marker SEQ-08-ACTIVITY.
memory_questions: What activity data does Memory store for agents?

steps.prompt: Step 9 of 20: Add an evaluation proof section explaining paired no-memory versus full-memory runs. Add exact marker SEQ-09-EVALS.
memory_questions: How do Memory eval comparisons work?

steps.prompt: Step 10 of 20: Add a Docker evaluation section explaining reproducible full-stack runs. Add exact marker SEQ-10-DOCKER.
memory_questions: How should Dockerized Memory evaluations be explained?

steps.prompt: Step 11 of 20: Add a token cost section showing why token capture matters. Add exact marker SEQ-11-TOKENS.
memory_questions: How should Memory evaluation reports describe token usage?

steps.prompt: Step 12 of 20: Add a get-up-to-speed section for new agents. Add exact marker SEQ-12-SPEED.
memory_questions: How should a new agent get up to speed in this project?

steps.prompt: Step 13 of 20: Add a memory types section covering plan, implementation, debugging, reference, and project memories. Add exact marker SEQ-13-TYPES.
memory_questions: What are the different Memory memory types used for?

steps.prompt: Step 14 of 20: Add a curation section explaining durable facts versus noisy logs. Add exact marker SEQ-14-CURATION.
memory_questions: How does Memory decide what should become durable memory?

steps.prompt: Step 15 of 20: Add a local-first/privacy section. Add exact marker SEQ-15-LOCAL.
memory_questions: What should users know about Memory being local-first?

steps.prompt: Step 16 of 20: Add a CLI and TUI section showing how agents and humans inspect Memory. Add exact marker SEQ-16-UI.
memory_questions: How do the Memory CLI and TUI help users inspect memory?

steps.prompt: Step 17 of 20: Add an architecture strip covering service, Postgres/pgvector, graph, and embeddings. Add exact marker SEQ-17-ARCH.
memory_questions: What are the main parts of the Memory architecture?

steps.prompt: Step 18 of 20: Add an interview-proof section explaining how to describe Memory evaluations. Add exact marker SEQ-18-INTERVIEW.
memory_questions: How should Memory evaluations be explained in a job interview?

steps.prompt: Step 19 of 20: Improve visual hierarchy, spacing, and responsive behavior. Add exact marker SEQ-19-POLISH.
memory_questions: What visual and documentation details matter for first impressions of Memory?

steps.prompt: Step 20 of 20: Add accessibility and final call-to-action details. Add exact marker SEQ-20-A11Y. Ensure the page remains dependency-free.
memory_questions: What should a polished Memory front page emphasize before release?
